steps.description: Audit and stabilize the existing web shell entry/configuration instead of rebuilding from scratch: verify the current Vite + React + Tailwind + TypeScript setup, preserve the existing query persistence setup that already avoids prior cascade errors, and minimally adjust only root wiring where it diverges from the requested shell behavior (public login route, protected app shell route tree, default redirects, and provider composition).

Refactor the shared auth and workspace infrastructure using the existing hub-safe files without changing their public surface unexpectedly: keep exported hook/provider names stable, ensure auth fetches GET /api/auth/me on mount, maintains session/user/logout/isSuperAdmin behavior, and make workspace switching/current workspace state align with the shell requirements while remaining compatible with existing consumers.

Normalize the shared API client so it remains the single typed fetch wrapper for the shell: preserve existing consumers, verify credentials are always included, fetch and cache CSRF tokens from /api/csrf-token for mutations, retry on CSRF failures, and expose typed responses used by auth/login/workspace/data contexts without widening breakage across the 50+ importing files.

Keep the protected routing boundary minimal and compatible: update the protected-route behavior to redirect unauthenticated users to /login exactly as requested for the frontend shell, removing legacy root-to-home behavior only if it conflicts with the requested route structure.

Revise the login page to match the requested shell login flow while preserving existing auth integrations: keep the email/password form, ensure CSRF-compatible login submission via the shared auth/api layer, and redirect successful logins to /docs unless an allowed return target is present.

Rework the root route map in the entrypoint to the requested shell structure, but do so incrementally to avoid the previous 744-error cascade: wire /login as public, / as protected AppLayout, and protected child routes for /docs, /documents/:id, /issues, /projects, /programs, /team/*, /settings, /dashboard, and /my-week while preserving existing page modules already present in the repo.

Trim and align the AppLayout shell to the requested frontend structure rather than replacing it wholesale: preserve existing reusable pieces where possible, ensure the left icon rail is 48px wide and includes Dashboard/Docs/Programs/Projects/Teams/Settings modes, keep the workspace switcher at the top, user avatar/logout at the bottom, semantic <nav>/<aside>/<main> landmarks, mode-driven contextual sidebar content, Cmd+K command palette stub behavior, session-timeout modal hookup, and ErrorBoundary around the outlet content.

Adapt the command palette to an intentional stub for the shell milestone so Cmd+K visibly opens a placeholder palette without forcing implementation of real commands yet, while keeping existing imports and component contracts compatible with AppLayout.

Bring the data contexts in line with the requested shell data-fetching infrastructure, but by layering on the existing TanStack Query hooks instead of replacing them: ensure DocumentsContext, IssuesContext, ProgramsContext, and ProjectsContext clearly expose list state plus create/update/delete mutation APIs expected by the shell and sidebar navigation.

Update route-aware shell consumers that may rely on old navigation assumptions so the revised shell continues compiling: adjust page modules or shell-related imports only where the new protected root/default redirects or AppLayout expectations require it, avoiding broad page-content rewrites.
Apply targeted test updates only to files that exercise login or shell-protection behavior so the revised shell remains covered without triggering unrelated failures.

tool_input.command: cd /Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship-rebuild-rerun-20260327c && grep -R "from '@/pages/\|from '@/components/\|from '@/contexts/\|from '@/hooks/\|from '@/lib/" web/src/main.tsx web/src/pages/App.tsx web/src/pages/Login.tsx web/src/h

messages.content:
Build the React + Vite + TailwindCSS frontend shell for Ship. This is the application layout with navigation, routing, auth context, and data-fetching infrastructure. Do not implement individual page content yet; focus on the shell that all pages render inside.

Set up the web package: Vite with React plugin, TailwindCSS with PostCSS, TypeScript, path alias `@/` pointing to `src/`. Create `src/main.tsx` with React Router, TanStack Query provider (with devtools), and the root route structure.

Create `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx`: auth context with login/logout/user state, fetches GET /api/auth/me on mount, stores session, provides `useAuth()` hook returning user/logout/isSuperAdmin. Create `src/contexts/WorkspaceContext.tsx` for current workspace and workspace switching. Create `src/lib/api.ts` as the shared fetch wrapper that auto-includes credentials, handles CSRF token (fetches from /api/csrf-token and attaches X-CSRF-Token header on mutations), and returns typed responses.

Create `src/pages/App.tsx` (the AppLayout component): icon rail on the left (48px wide) with mode icons for Dashboard/Docs/Programs/Projects/Teams/Settings, workspace switcher dropdown at top, user avatar with logout at bottom. Collapsible left sidebar (224px) showing context-specific lists (documents tree, issues list, projects list, programs list, team sidebar) based on active mode. Main content area renders `<Outlet />`. Wire up Cmd+K for command palette (stub). Add session timeout modal via `useSessionTimeout` hook. Include `<ErrorBoundary>` around the main content. Use semantic HTML landmarks: `<nav>` for rail, `<aside>` for sidebar, `<main>` for content.

Create `src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx` that redirects to /login if not authenticated. Create `src/pages/Login.tsx` with email/password form, CSRF token handling, and redirect to /docs on success. Set up routes: /login (public), / (protected, renders AppLayout with children: /docs, /documents/:id, /issues, /projects, /programs, /team/*, /settings, /dashboard, /my-week).

Create data contexts: `src/contexts/DocumentsContext.tsx` (TanStack Query for documents list, createDocument/updateDocument/deleteDocument mutations), `src/contexts/IssuesContext.tsx`, `src/contexts/ProgramsContext.tsx`, `src/contexts/ProjectsContext.tsx` following the same pattern.

Verify: run `pnpm dev`, navigate to localhost:5173, see login page, log in with seeded user, confirm icon rail + sidebar + main content area render, confirm switching modes updates the sidebar, confirm Cmd+K opens command palette stub, confirm logout redirects to login.

Instruction: Build the React + Vite + TailwindCSS frontend shell for Ship. This is the application layout with navigation, routing, auth context, and data-fetching infrastructure. Do not implement individual page content yet; focus on the shell that all pages render inside.

Set up the web package: Vite with React plugin, TailwindCSS with PostCSS, TypeScript, path alias `@/` pointing to `src/`. Create `src/main.tsx` with React Router, TanStack Query provider (with devtools), and the root route structure.

Create `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx`: auth context with login/logout/user state, fetches GET /api/auth/me on mount, stores session, provides `useAuth()` hook returning user/logout/isSuperAdmin. Create `src/contexts/WorkspaceContext.tsx` for current workspace and workspace switching. Create `src/lib/api.ts` as the shared fetch wrapper that auto-includes credentials, handles CSRF token (fetches from /api/csrf-token and attaches X-CSRF-Token header on mutations), and returns typed responses.

Create `src/pages/App.tsx` (the AppLayout component): icon rail on the left (48px wide) with mode icons for Dashboard/Docs/Programs/Projects/Teams/Settings, workspace switcher dropdown at top, user avatar with logout at bottom. Collapsible left sidebar (224px) showing context-specific lists (documents tree, issues list, projects list, programs list, team sidebar) based on active mode. Main content area renders `<Outlet />`. Wire up Cmd+K for command palette (stub). Add session timeout modal via `useSessionTimeout` hook. Include `<ErrorBoundary>` around the main content. Use semantic HTML landmarks: `<nav>` for rail, `<aside>` for sidebar, `<main>` for content.

Create `src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx` that redirects to /login if not authenticated. Create `src/pages/Login.tsx` with email/password form, CSRF token handling, and redirect to /docs on success. Set up routes: /login (public), / (protected, renders AppLayout with children: /docs, /documents/:id, /issues, /projects, /programs, /team/*, /settings, /dashboard, /my-week).

Create data contexts: `src/contexts/DocumentsContext.tsx` (TanStack Query for documents list, createDocument/updateDocument/deleteDocument mutations), `src/contexts/IssuesContext.tsx`, `src/contexts/ProgramsContext.tsx`, `src/contexts/ProjectsContext.tsx` following the same pattern.

Verify: run `pnpm dev`, navigate to localhost:5173, see login page, log in with seeded user, confirm icon rail + sidebar + main content area render, confirm switching modes updates the sidebar, confirm Cmd+K opens command palette stub, confirm logout redirects to login.
